items.prompt: Which text operation splits raw strings into words or subwords before tagging?
items.explain: Tokenization is the prerequisite step for most text taggers.

items.prompt: In Example 4.12, which entity types appear?
choices: High, medium, and low value bands
items.explain: Apple is typed as an organization and Cupertino as a location.

items.prompt: Segmentation masks typically provide tighter object boundaries than axis-aligned bounding boxes.
items.explain: Pixel- or polygon-level labels capture fine boundaries important in medical and scene parsing tasks.

items.prompt: Which image format places keypoints on eyes, nose, and mouth corners?
items.explain: Landmarking supports face alignment and pose estimation.

items.prompt: List two text annotation building blocks besides tokenization.
items.rubric: Any two of sentiment tagging, NER, POS tagging, aspect tags.
items.sample_answer: Sentiment tagging and named entity recognition; part-of-speech tagging is also valid.
